question.text: What is the vision for Claude's new Dispatch feature?

context.raw: Dispatch to Claude and check in from anywhere—a task, a code session, in one continuous thread. Your phone is like a walkie talkie that can communicate with Claude on your computer. Just send Claude a message, and it'll work on tasks using your computer. Claude can also run tasks on a schedule or whenever you need them. Remember to keep your computer awake so Claude can keep working. Claude will access your desktop (files, apps, browser) to complete tasks you send from your phone. This may have security risks. Only pair devices that you own and trust.

response.summary: Cross-device, asynchronous task execution — send and monitor Claude tasks from your phone while Claude works on your desktop.

pillars.pillar: Walkie-talkie metaphor. Send messages from your phone, Claude executes on your computer with full desktop access (files, apps, browser).
One persistent conversation thread spanning devices and contexts (tasks, code sessions, check-ins).
Claude can run tasks on a schedule or when triggered, not just in real-time interactive sessions.
Runs on actual computer with access to full environment, not a sandboxed API.

tradeoffs.tradeoff: Computer must stay awake for Claude to keep working.
Security surface — granting remote access to desktop. Trust model: only pair devices you own.

response.insight: Essentially turns Claude Code into a persistent agent daemon that can be dispatched work from anywhere, rather than requiring terminal presence.

question.text: Decompose Dispatch and Channels into ASCII wireframes showing architecture, data flow, feature taxonomy, and how canonical features relate.

context.source: Official Anthropic documentation — Channels (Automation) page + comparison table
context.raw: Channels are MCP servers that push events into a running Claude Code session. Two-way: Claude reads event, replies back through the same channel (chat bridge). Events only arrive while session is open — background process or persistent terminal for always-on. Supported: Telegram, Discord, iMessage (research preview, requires Bun). Security: sender allowlist per channel, pairing codes, --channels flag per session. Enterprise: channelsEnabled master switch, allowedChannelPlugins whitelist. Comparison matrix: Web sessions (cloud sandbox), Slack (spawns web session), MCP (pull/query), Remote Control (drive local from claude.ai/mobile), Channels (push events into local session).

response.summary: Five ASCII wireframes decomposing the full Dispatch + Channels architecture: system topology, channel data flow, feature taxonomy, security layers, and the canonical feature comparison matrix.

wireframe.name: Channel Data Flow — Bidirectional Message Path
wireframe.ascii: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ CHANNEL DATA FLOW (Bidirectional) │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ INBOUND (Push → Session) │ │ ════════════════════════ │ │ │ │ Sender ──► Platform API ──► Channel Plugin ──► MCP Event │ │ (you) (Telegram) (Bun script) <channel │ │ source="tg"> │ │ │ │ │ ┌─────▼──────┐ │ │ │ Allowlist │ │ │ │ Check │──► DROP (silent) │ │ │ sender_id │ if not paired │ │ │ ∈ allowed? │ │ │ └─────┬──────┘ │ │ │ ✓ │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ Claude Code │ │ │ │ Agent Loop │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Reads event │ │ │ │ Does work │ │ │ │ (files, tools) │ │ │ └────────┬─────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ OUTBOUND (Session → Reply) │ │ │ ══════════════════════════ │ │ │ ▼ │ │ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ reply() tool │ │ │ │ call via same │ │ │ │ channel plugin │ │ │ └────────┬─────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ ▼ │ │ Terminal shows: Platform shows: │ │ ┌──────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────┐ │ │ │ tool_use: reply │ │ Claude: "Here's │ │ │ │ status: "sent" │ │ what I found..." │ │ │ │ (no reply text) │ │ (full response) │ │ │ └──────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ PERMISSION RELAY (optional) │ │ ═══════════════════════════ │ │ │ │ Claude hits permission prompt ──► Channel forwards to phone │ │ Session PAUSES ◄── You approve/deny remotely │ │ Session RESUMES │ │ │ │ ⚠️ Anyone on allowlist can approve/deny via relay! │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

wireframe.name: Feature Taxonomy — Dispatch vs Channels vs Canonical Features
wireframe.ascii: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ FEATURE TAXONOMY TREE │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Claude Code Connectivity │ │ │ │ │ ├── LOCAL (you're at the terminal) │ │ │ ├── Interactive REPL session │ │ │ ├── Standard MCP servers (pull/query model) │ │ │ └── Scheduled Tasks (CronCreate — poll on timer) │ │ │ │ │ ├── REMOTE CONTROL (you drive, Claude executes locally) │ │ │ ├── claude.ai web ──► local session via WebSocket │ │ │ ├── Claude mobile app ──► local session │ │ │ └── ≈ "Dispatch" brand: phone as walkie-talkie │ │ │ │ │ ├── CHANNELS (events pushed TO your session) │ │ │ ├── Chat Bridges (bidirectional) │ │ │ │ ├── Telegram ──► session ──► Telegram reply │ │ │ │ ├── Discord ──► session ──► Discord reply │ │ │ │ └── iMessage ──► session ──► iMessage reply │ │ │ ├── Webhook Receivers (unidirectional push) │ │ │ │ ├── CI/CD results │ │ │ │ ├── Error tracker alerts │ │ │ │ └── Deploy pipeline events │ │ │ └── Custom Channels (build your own MCP server) │ │ │ │ │ ├── CLOUD (fresh sandbox, async) │ │ │ ├── Claude Code on the Web (GitHub clone → sandbox) │ │ │ └── Claude in Slack (@Claude mention → web session) │ │ │ │ │ └── ENTERPRISE CONTROLS │ │ ├── channelsEnabled (master switch, default: off) │ │ ├── allowedChannelPlugins (whitelist replacement) │ │ └── Per-session opt-in (--channels flag) │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ KEY DISTINCTION │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Remote Control = YOU push commands TO Claude │ │ │ │ Channels = WORLD pushes events TO Claude │ │ │ │ Dispatch = Brand umbrella for both │ │ │ │ Cloud sessions = Separate sandbox, no local files │ │ │ │ MCP servers = Claude pulls FROM them (on-demand) │ │ │ │ Scheduled = Claude polls ON timer (cron) │ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

wireframe.name: Security Architecture — Layers of Defense
wireframe.ascii: ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ SECURITY ARCHITECTURE │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ LAYER 1: Organization Policy (Team/Enterprise) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ channelsEnabled: true/false ◄── Admin console toggle │ │ │ │ allowedChannelPlugins: [...] ◄── Plugin whitelist │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ OFF by default. Pro/Max skip this layer entirely. │ │ │ └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ ✓ enabled │ │ ▼ │ │ LAYER 2: Session Opt-in (User) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ claude --channels plugin:telegram@official │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Only named plugins activate. Being in .mcp.json is NOT │ │ │ │ enough — must be in --channels flag. │ │ │ └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ ✓ opted in │ │ ▼ │ │ LAYER 3: Plugin Allowlist (Anthropic / Org) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Plugin must be on effective allowlist: │ │ │ │ - Anthropic default list (if org hasn't overridden) │ │ │ │ - OR org's allowedChannelPlugins list │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ --dangerously-load-development-channels bypasses this │ │ │ └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ ✓ approved │ │ ▼ │ │ LAYER 4: Sender Allowlist (Per-Channel) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Each channel maintains its own sender allowlist: │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Telegram/Discord: │ │ │ │ 1. Send message to bot → get pairing code │ │ │ │ 2. /telegram:access pair <code> │ │ │ │ 3. /telegram:access policy allowlist │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ iMessage: │ │ │ │ - Texting yourself = auto-allowed │ │ │ │ - Others: /imessage:access allow <handle> │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Unknown sender_id → SILENT DROP │ │ │ └─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ ✓ paired │ │ ▼ │ │ LAYER 5: Permission Prompts (Runtime) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Claude hits tool permission → SESSION PAUSES │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Options: │ │ │ │ a) Wait for terminal user to approve │ │ │ │ b) Permission relay via channel (if declared) │ │ │ │ ⚠️ Allowlisted sender can approve/deny remotely │ │ │ │ c) --dangerously-skip-permissions (unattended, risky) │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

wireframe.name: Canonical Feature Comparison Matrix
wireframe.ascii:
┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ CANONICAL FEATURE COMPARISON MATRIX │ ├────────────────┬──────────┬───────────┬──────────┬──────────┬─────────────┤ │ │ Web │ Slack │ MCP │ Remote │ Channels │ │ │ Sessions │ │ Servers │ Control │ │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Direction │ async │ trigger → │ pull ← │ push → │ push → │ │ │ dispatch │ cloud │ on-demand│ to local │ to local │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Environment │ cloud │ cloud │ local │ local │ local │ │ │ sandbox │ sandbox │ │ │ │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Initiator │ you │ team │ Claude │ you │ world │ │ │ │ (@Claude) │ (query) │ (phone) │ (events) │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Session │ fresh │ fresh │ existing │ existing │ existing │ │ │ clone │ spawn │ │ │ │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ File Access │ GitHub │ GitHub │ defined │ full │ full │ │ │ clone │ clone │ by server│ desktop │ desktop │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Always-on? │ yes │ yes │ no │ no │ only while │ │ │ (cloud) │ (cloud) │ (idle) │ (active) │ session open│ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Bidirectional? │ no │ thread │ req/resp │ yes │ yes │ │ │ (check │ reply │ │ │ (chat │ │ │ later) │ │ │ │ bridge) │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Use Case │ delegate │ team │ give │ steer │ react to │ │ │ async │ collab │ Claude │ session │ external │ │ │ work │ │ data │ remotely │ events │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Auth Model │ GitHub │ Slack │ per- │ claude │ allowlist + │ │ │ + claude │ + claude │ server │ .ai auth │ pairing │ ├────────────────┼──────────┼───────────┼──────────┼──────────┼─────────────┤ │ Enterprise │ org │ org │ .mcp.json│ managed │ channels │ │ Control │ policy │ policy │ managed │ settings │ Enabled + │ │ │ │ │ │ │ plugin list │ └────────────────┴──────────┴───────────┴──────────┴──────────┴─────────────┘
DATA FLOW PATTERNS:
Web Sessions: You ═══► Cloud Sandbox ═══► Results (check later) Slack: @Claude ═══► Cloud Sandbox ═══► Thread reply MCP Servers: Claude ◄═══ Query ═══► Server (on-demand pull) Remote Control: Phone ═══► Local Session ═══► Desktop actions Channels: World ═══► Channel Plugin ═══► Local Session ═══► Reply Sched. Tasks: Timer ═══► Claude polls ═══► React (cron-based)

response.insight: Dispatch is the brand umbrella. Remote Control = you commanding Claude. Channels = the world notifying Claude. Together they make the local session a persistent, event-driven agent that both you and external systems can interact with — but only while the machine is awake and the session is open. Cloud features (Web, Slack) trade local file access for always-on availability.

question.text: Improve and extend the canonical wireframes with Scheduled Tasks (/loop, CronCreate, Cloud, Desktop), the full Platforms and Integrations matrix, and the "work when away" taxonomy from official docs.

context.source: Official Anthropic documentation — Scheduled Tasks page + Platforms and Integrations page
context.raw:
SCHEDULED TASKS: - /loop skill: quickest way to schedule recurring prompt. Interval syntax: 30m, 2h, default 10m. - CronCreate/CronList/CronDelete tools. 5-field cron expressions. Session-scoped, max 50 tasks. - 3-day auto-expiry on recurring tasks. Jitter: recurring up to 10% late (cap 15min), one-shot up to 90s early. - Three scheduling tiers: Cloud (Anthropic infra, persistent, no local files, 1h min), Desktop (local, persistent, 1min), /loop (session-scoped, local, 1min). - CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_CRON=1 to disable.
PLATFORMS: - CLI: full feature set, Agent SDK, headless, third-party providers - Desktop: diff viewer, app preview, computer use, Dispatch - VS Code: inline diffs, integrated terminal - JetBrains: diff viewer, selection sharing - Web: Anthropic cloud, tasks continue after disconnect
INTEGRATIONS: - Chrome: browser automation with logged-in sessions - GitHub Actions: CI pipeline (PR reviews, issue triage, scheduled) - GitLab CI/CD: same for GitLab - Code Review: automatic PR review - Slack: @Claude mentions → cloud sessions - MCP servers + connectors: Linear, Notion, Google Drive, internal APIs
WORK WHEN AWAY (5 modes): - Dispatch: phone → Desktop session (pair mobile app) - Remote Control: drive running session from claude.ai/mobile - Channels: push events from chat/servers into CLI session - Slack: @Claude → cloud session - Scheduled Tasks: cron across CLI/Desktop/Cloud

response.summary: Eight extended wireframes: improved system topology with scheduling, scheduling tier architecture, /loop lifecycle, complete platform matrix, integrations map, work-when-away decision tree, unified connectivity model, and the definitive feature comparison with all dimensions.

wireframe.name: Extended System Topology — All Connectivity Modes
Scheduling Tier Architecture — /loop vs Desktop vs Cloud
wireframe.ascii: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ SCHEDULING TIER ARCHITECTURE │ ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ TIER 1: /loop (Session-Scoped) TIER 2: Desktop Scheduled │ │ ═══════════════════════════ ═══════════════════════════ │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────────────────┐ ┌──────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ /loop 5m check deploy │ │ Desktop App → Schedule │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ CronCreate │ │ │ │ Persistent daemon │ │ │ │ │ │ 5-field cron expr │ │ │ │ Survives restarts │ │ │ │ │ │ 8-char task ID │ │ │ │ Configurable perms │ │ │ │ │ └────────┬───────────┘ │ │ └────────┬───────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ▼ │ │ ▼ │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ Scheduler (1s tick)│ │ │ │ Local scheduler │ │ │ │ │ │ Low-priority queue │ │ │ │ Background process │ │ │ │ │ │ Fires between turns│ │ │ │ Full desktop access│ │ │ │ │ └────────┬───────────┘ │ │ └────────┬───────────┘ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Constraints: │ │ Constraints: │ │ │ │ • Session must be open │ │ • Machine must be on │ │ │ │ • 3-day auto-expiry │ │ • No session required │ │ │ │ • Max 50 tasks │ │ • Persistent across │ │ │ │ • No catch-up on miss │ │ restarts │ │ │ │ • Min interval: 1min │ │ • Min interval: 1min │ │ │ │ • Jitter applied │ │ • MCP via config files │ │ │ │ • DISABLE_CRON=1 kills │ │ + connectors │ │ │ └──────────────────────────┘ └──────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ TIER 3: Cloud Scheduled │ │ ═══════════════════════ │ │ │ │ ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ Anthropic-Managed Infrastructure │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────────┐ ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ │ │ /schedule in CLI │ │ Always-on (no machine needed) │ │ │ │ │ │ or web config │ │ Fresh GitHub clone each run │ │ │ │ │ └────────────────────┘ │ Connectors configured per task │ │ │ │ │ │ No permission prompts │ │ │ │ │ Constraints: │ Min interval: 1 hour │ │ │ │ │ • No local file access │ Persistent across everything │ │ │ │ │ • No desktop tools └─────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ • Runs autonomously │ │ │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ │ DECISION FLOW: │ │ │ │ Need local files? ──NO──► Cloud Scheduled (always-on, 1h min) │ │ │ │ │ YES │ │ │ │ │ Need persistence? ──NO──► /loop (session-scoped, quick) │ │ │ │ │ YES │ │ │ │ │ └──► Desktop Scheduled (local, persistent, 1min) │ │ │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

wireframe.name: /loop Lifecycle — From Command to Cron Fire
Platform Matrix — Where Claude Code Runs
Work When Away — Decision Tree
wireframe.ascii: ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ WORK WHEN AWAY — DECISION TREE │ ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ "I need Claude to work when I'm not at my terminal" │ │ │ │ What triggers it? │ │ │ │ │ ├── ME (on demand, from phone/browser) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ├── New task, don't care about local files? │ │ │ │ └──► DISPATCH (phone → Desktop session) │ │ │ │ Setup: pair mobile app with Desktop │ │ │ │ Runs on: your machine (Desktop) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ └── Steer an EXISTING session? │ │ │ └──► REMOTE CONTROL (phone/web → running session) │ │ │ Setup: claude remote-control │ │ │ Runs on: your machine (CLI or VS Code) │ │ │ │ │ ├── MY TEAM (from chat) │ │ │ └──► SLACK (@Claude in channel → cloud session → PR) │ │ │ Setup: install Slack app + enable web sessions │ │ │ Runs on: Anthropic cloud │ │ │ │ │ ├── THE WORLD (external events) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ ├── From chat apps (Telegram/Discord/iMessage)? │ │ │ │ └──► CHANNELS (push into running CLI session) │ │ │ │ Setup: install channel plugin + pair │ │ │ │ Runs on: your machine (CLI, session must be open) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ └── From CI/webhooks/monitoring? │ │ │ └──► CHANNELS (custom MCP server pushes events) │ │ │ Setup: build your own channel │ │ │ Runs on: your machine (CLI, session must be open) │ │ │ │ │ └── TIME (recurring schedule) │ │ │ │ │ ├── Quick poll during this session? │ │ │ └──► /loop (session-scoped, dies on exit) │ │ │ Min: 1min, expires: 3 days │ │ │ │ │ ├── Need local files + persistence? │ │ │ └──► DESKTOP SCHEDULED (survives restart) │ │ │ Min: 1min, configurable permissions │ │ │ │ │ └── Don't need local files, must be reliable? │ │ └──► CLOUD SCHEDULED (Anthropic infra, always-on) │ │ Min: 1 hour, GitHub clone each run │ │ │ │ ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ │ TRADEOFF AXES │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Reliability ◄─────────────────────────────────────► Locality │ │ │ │ (Cloud: always on, (Local: full desktop, │ │ │ │ no machine needed) real files, MCP servers) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Reactive ◄────────────────────────────────────────► Proactive │ │ │ │ (Channels: world (Scheduled: Claude │ │ │ │ pushes events) polls on timer) │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Interactive ◄─────────────────────────────────────► Autonomous │ │ │ │ (Remote Control: (Dispatch/Cloud: │ │ │ │ you steer in fire and check later) │ │ │ │ real-time) │ │ │ └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

wireframe.name: Definitive Feature Comparison — All Dimensions
wireframe.ascii:
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ DEFINITIVE FEATURE COMPARISON — ALL DIMENSIONS │ ├──────────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬──────────┬────────────────┤ │ │ Dispatch │ Remote │ Channels │ Slack │ Web │ Scheduled │ │ │ │ Control │ │ │ Sessions │ Tasks │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Trigger │ you │ you │ world │ team │ you │ time │ │ │ (phone) │ (phone/ │ (events) │ (@Claude)│ (web UI) │ (cron) │ │ │ │ web) │ │ │ │ │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Runs on │ Desktop │ CLI / │ CLI │ Anthropic│ Anthropic│ CLI / Desktop /│ │ │ (local) │ VS Code │ (local) │ cloud │ cloud │ Cloud │ │ │ │ (local) │ │ │ │ │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Session │ new │ existing │ existing │ new │ new │ depends on tier│ │ │ │ │ │ (cloud) │ (sandbox)│ │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Direction │ you ──► │ you ──► │ world──► │ team ──► │ you ──► │ timer ──► │ │ │ Claude │ Claude │ Claude │ cloud │ sandbox │ Claude │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Local files │ YES │ YES │ YES │ NO │ NO │ /loop: YES │ │ │ │ │ │ (clone) │ (clone) │ Desktop: YES │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Cloud: NO │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Machine │ YES │ YES │ YES │ NO │ NO │ /loop: YES │ │ must be on │ │ │ │ │ │ Desktop: YES │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Cloud: NO │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Session │ NO │ YES │ YES │ NO │ NO │ /loop: YES │ │ must be open │ (spawns) │ │ │ │ │ Desktop: NO │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ Cloud: NO │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Persistent │ YES │ NO │ NO │ YES │ YES │ /loop: NO │ │ across │ (Desktop │ (session │ (session │ (cloud) │ (cloud) │ Desktop: YES │ │ restarts │ manages)│ bound) │ bound) │ │ │ Cloud: YES │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Bidir reply │ YES │ YES │ YES │ thread │ NO │ NO │ │ │ (thread) │ (live) │ (bridge) │ reply │(check │ (fire & forget)│ │ │ │ │ │ │ later) │ │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Setup │ pair │ claude │ plugin + │ Slack │ enable │ /loop: none │ │ effort │ mobile │ remote- │ pair + │ app + │ web │ Desktop: config│ │ │ app │ control │ --channel│ web │ sessions │ Cloud: /schedule│ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Min interval │ n/a │ n/a │ n/a │ n/a │ n/a │ /loop: 1min │ │ │ (on- │ (on- │ (event- │ (event- │ (on- │ Desktop: 1min │ │ │ demand) │ demand) │ driven) │ driven) │ demand) │ Cloud: 1hr │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Enterprise │ Desktop │ managed │ channels │ org │ org │ per tier │ │ controls │ policy │ settings │ Enabled +│ policy │ policy │ controls │ │ │ │ │ plugin │ │ │ │ │ │ │ │ whitelist│ │ │ │ ├──────────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼──────────┼────────────────┤ │ Best for │ delegate │ steer │ react to │ team │ delegate │ recurring │ │ │ from │ in-prog │ external │ collab │ async │ automation │ │ │ phone │ work │ events │ from chat│ work │ polling │ └──────────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴──────────┴────────────────┘
COMPLETE DATA FLOW PATTERNS:
Dispatch: Phone ═══► Desktop (new session) ═══► Work ═══► Reply in thread Remote Control: Phone/Web ═══► Existing Session ═══► You drive live Channels: World ═══► Channel Plugin ═══► Existing Session ═══► Reply via platform Slack: @Claude ═══► Anthropic Cloud ═══► New Session ═══► Thread reply + PR Web Sessions: You ═══► Cloud Sandbox (GH clone) ═══► Results (check later) /loop: Timer ═══► Existing Session (between turns) ═══► React Desktop Sched: Timer ═══► Desktop daemon ═══► New/existing session ═══► React Cloud Sched: Timer ═══► Anthropic infra (fresh clone) ═══► React autonomously MCP Servers: Claude ◄═══► Server (on-demand pull, not push) Chrome: Claude ═══► Browser (your sessions) ═══► Automate sites CI/CD: Pipeline trigger ═══► Claude in GH Actions/GitLab ═══► PR/review

response.insight: The complete Claude Code connectivity model has four paradigms: DRIVE (you command), PUSH (world notifies), PULL (Claude queries), SCHEDULE (time triggers). Dispatch and Remote Control are both DRIVE but differ in session lifecycle (new vs existing). Channels and Slack are both PUSH but differ in runtime (local vs cloud). Scheduled Tasks span all three runtimes (session/local/cloud) on the SCHEDULE axis. The fundamental tradeoff is locality vs reliability: local gives desktop access, cloud gives always-on resilience.
